ocr.text: In the rapidly evolving landscape of the Asia-Pacific financial sector, cross-border remittance flows have seen significant shifts,
driven by innovations in fintech and the adoption of digital payment solutions.
Cross-borderRemittanceData  Year  Amount(SGD)  GrowthRate
2019  500M  N/A  BaseYear  2020
550M  10%  [HIDDEN]  Year-on-Year  2021
600M  9%  SlightGrowth  Year-on-Year  2022
650M  [HIDDEN]  Stable  [HIDDEN]  2023
The introduction of open banking APIs has facilitated greater access to financial services, enabling users to engage in
transactions with enhanced efficiency. As indicated in Table 1, the remittance flows have stabilized, with the 2021 metric
showing a year-on-year comparison that reflects a solid growth trajectory.
Year  Revenue(SGD)  NetIncome(SGD)  CAPEX(SGD)  APITransactions
OpenBankingMetrics  CustomerGrowth(%)  Avg.TransactionValue  TotalAPIs  APIUsage(%)
[HIDDEN]  100M  [HIDDEN]  10M  2M  [HIDDEN]
Notably, the metric for 500M in 2021 indicates an 8% increase, illustrating the expanding market for digital financial services.
Year  Amount($M)  YoYChange(%)  MarketShare(%)  NewLoansIssued  DefaultRate(%)
2022  150  [HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  0.8
Additionally, the lending platform origination data showcases a robust upward trend, with a notable increase in users, as
evidenced by the jump from 10M to 20M in 2020. This growth is further supported by the rising percentage of consumers
engaging in these services, with 25% of respondents adopting new platforms, which has increased to 35% in subsequent years.
Such trends underline the critical role of fintech innovations in transforming the financial landscape across the region,
positioning firms like Revolut at the forefront of this digital evolution.
